CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Continued To Feb-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Dismiss For Inconvenient Forum, Or Alternatively, To Stay Based On Comity
Off Calendar May-22-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PETER A. ARHANGELSKY OF THE LAW FIRM OF EMROD & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-202-466-6937 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. CHAD PEHRSON O THE LAW FIRM OF PARR BROWN GEE & LOVELESS, TEL. NO. 1-801-532-7840 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Wakaya Perfection, LLC and Todd Smith's motion to dismiss for inconvenient forum is denied and their alternate motion to stay based on comity is granted. This case is stayed pending the resolution of the case in the Southern District of California. Wakaya and Mr. Smith have not met their burden of showing that this court is a seriously inconvenient forum or that the public and private factors favor the resolution of this case in Utah. There is a strong presumption in favor of plaintiff Youngevity International Corp.'s choice of forum because it is a California resident. Not all evidence or witnesses are in Utah, the defendants have not established that this case will turn on the participation of Utah witnesses, and the defendants' participation in the federal action in the Southern District of California demonstrates that it would not be inconvenient for them to participate in a lawsuit in California. Perhaps most significantly, California has a greater interest than Utah in adjudicating Proposition 65 claims affecting California residents. The pending case in the Southern District of California was filed first and encompasses the key issues raised in the complaint filed in this court: the arsenic and lead content in Wakaya's products and Wakaya's notice of the alleged dangers posed by its products to consumers. This overlap in subject matter is further shown by the similar discovery requests propounded in both cases. Resolution of the federal court case may determine some or all of the issues raised by the claims filed in this court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Youngevity International Corp.'s application for the pro hac vice admission of Eric Awerbuch is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. (Eric J. Awerbuch of Emrod & Associates, 2730 S Val Vista Dr. Bldg. 6, Ste. 133, Gilbert AZ 85295) Moving party to prepare form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
